---
title-block: false

format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-expand: true
    toc-location: left
    anchor-sections: false
    embed-resources: true
    css: styles.css
    echo: false
    warning: false
    message: false
    grid: 
      body-width: 1250px
---

```{r}
#
if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")

pacman::p_load(here,
               tidyverse,
               tidyr,
               rmarkdown,
               quarto,
               bsicons,
               rlang,
               glue,
               janitor,
               readxl,
               sf,
               knitr,
               kableExtra,
               colorRamps,
               extrafont,
               ggnewscale,
               plotly,
               leaflet)
```


```{r}
#
options(knitr.kable.NA = '--')

here::i_am("Code/sitrep_03.qmd")
```


```{r}
# Date of report generation
datetime_update <- paste0("data as of ", format(Sys.Date(), format = "%d/%m/%Y"))
filedate        <- format(Sys.Date(), format = "%Y%m%d")

# Date and start time of common event
date_event     <- lubridate::ymd("2026-02-15")
datetime_event <- lubridate::ymd_hms(paste0("2026-02-15", "15:00:00"))

# x-axis limits for epi curves (dates only)
date_epicurve_start <- lubridate::ymd(date_event - days(3))
date_epicurve_end   <- Sys.Date() + 2

# x-axis limits for epi curves (dates and times)
datetime_epicurve_start <- lubridate::ymd_hms(paste0("2026-02-15", "00:00:00"))
datetime_epicurve_end   <- lubridate::ymd_hms(paste0("2026-02-20", "00:00:00"))

# Menu items
menu_platter <- c("platter_prosciutto", "platter_bresaola", "platter_cheddar", "platter_blue", 
                  "platter_cornichons", "platter_quince", "platter_lavosh", "platter_bark")
menu_pizza   <- c("pizza_margherita", "pizza_pumpkin", "pizza_ham", "pizza_chicken", "pizza_prosciutto")
menu_fruit   <- c("fruit_grapes", "fruit_strawberries", "fruit_watermelon", "fruit_pineapple", 
                  "fruit_blueberries", "fruit_raspberries")
menu_dessert <- c("dessert_tart", "dessert_cake", "dessert_crackle", "dessert_donut", "sorbet_orange", 
                  "sorbet_passionfruit", "sorbet_raspberry", "gelato_honey", "gelato_chocolate", "gelato_vanilla")
menu_other   <- c("kids_chicken", "kids_fish", "chips", "drink_juice", "drink_other")
```


```{r}
# Read in functions
source(here::here("Code", "Functions", "f_menu_analyses.R"))
```


```{r}
# Read in and wrangle data
source(here::here("Code", "data_prep.R"))
```


```{r}
#| out-width: "100%"
knitr::include_graphics(here::here("Images", "nephu_logo.png"))
```

# Situation Report [#3]{.red-text}

## Outbreak {.unnumbered .unlisted}

<div style="height: 0.5em;"></div>

*Information contained in this Sit Rep is sensitive, confidential, and not intended for wider distribution or use beyond the PH-IMT.*

<div style="height: 0.5em;"></div>

::: {.incident-table}
| Incident name             | Studley Park Boathouse [norovirus outbreak]{.red-text}            |
|:--------------------------|:------------------------------------------------------------------|
| PHESS ID                  | 320266223985                                                      |
| Date/time Sit Rep updated | [`r format(Sys.Date(), format = "%d/%m/%Y")`]{.red-text}          |
| Sit Rep approved by (IC)  | [Claire Gordon]{.red-text}                                        |
| Sit Rep prepared by (IO)  | Alana Little                                                      |

: {tbl-colwidths="[35,65]"}
:::

<div style="height: 1em;"></div>

::: {.callout-note title="Overall summary of incident" icon=false collapse=false}
[NEPHU received a report from a member of the public that multiple people had become unwell with gastroenteritis following a birthday party held at Studley Park Boathouse in Kew on 15/02/2026. Case interviews and a Microsoft Forms survey disseminated to the party attendees via the party host identified 26 symptomatic and nine non-symptomatic attendees.]{.red-text}  

[No clear common food sources were identified and the epidemiology was suggestive of person-to-person spread of viral gastroenteritis. Norovirus was identified in six faecal samples from cases across three households. Boroondara Council conducted a site visit to the venue and identified no food safety or hygiene concerns.]{.red-text} 

[The most recent case had a symptom onset of 24/02/2026. No further cases have been identified associated with the party or with any other events held at the venue, and the outbreak was declared closed on 04/03/2026.]{.red-text}

- Report from a member of the public received by NEPHU after hours on 17/02/2026
  - Multiple unwell people who had attended a birthday party at Studley Park Boathouse on 15/02/2026
  - Food mostly catered by Boathouse, additional items brought in by attendees
  - Approximately 41/75 attendees unwell with gastroenteritis symptoms

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Interviews conducted with 22 attendees across seven households
  - 16/21 interviewed attendees symptomatic
  - Most common symptoms: nausea (17/26, 65%), vomiting (17/26, 65%), and lethargy (14/26, 54%)

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- No faecal samples collected at the time of outbreak notification
  - Seven interviewed attendees across four households have agreed to provide faecal samples, relevant Councils arranging sample pot delivery
  - Six faecal samples from three different households have been sent to MDU
  - All six samples are negative for bacterial pathogens [and positive for norovirus]{.red-text}

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Menu-based questionnaire distributed to birthday party attendees via party host on 20/02/2026
  - Responses received from 13 attendees as of `r format(Sys.Date(), format = "%d/%m/%Y")`
  - 10/13 survey respondents symptomatic
  - [No clear food source identified from interviews or survey responses]{.red-text}

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Boroondara Council notified 18/02/2026 and conducted site visit 19/02/2026
  - No ill food handlers or staff reported
  - No leftover food available for sampling
  - Deep clean carried out by external cleaning company evening of 18/02/2026
  - Environmental swabbing conducted 20/02/2026
  - [As norovirus has been identified as the causative agent and no bacterial pathogens were isolated from cases, further testing of the environmental swabs will not be required]{.red-text}
  
<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- [The most recent case’s symptom onset was on 24/02/2026]{.red-text}
  - [If no additional cases identified with symptom onset after this date, outbreak eligible for closure on 28/02/2026 (2x 48hr incubation period for norovirus)]{.red-text}

:::

<div style="height: 0.125em;"></div>

::: {.callout-tip title="Outbreak case definitions" icon=false collapse=false}
**Confirmed case:** Individuals with a clinically compatible illness who attended the birthday party at Studley Park Boathouse on 15/02/2026 or who have an epidemiological link to a confirmed case.
:::

***

## Epidemiological Investigations

- Report from a member of the public received by NEPHU after hours on 17/02/2026
  - Multiple unwell people who had attended a birthday party at Studley Park Boathouse on 15/02/2026
  - Approximately 41/75 attendees unwell with gastroenteritis symptoms

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- 26 symptomatic attendees and nine non-symptomatic attendees have been identified through interviews and survey responses
  - Interviews conducted with 16 symptomatic attendees and six non-symptomatic attendees
  - As of 24/02/2026, survey responses have been received from 10 additional symptomatic attendees and three non-symptomatic attendees

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Two symptomatic survey respondents have also completed an interview
  - Five survey respondents were anonymous, and it is unknown whether these respondents also completed an interview

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- The most common symptoms reported by symptomatic attendees were nausea (17/26, 65%), vomiting (17/26, 65%), and lethargy (14/26, 54%)
  - Noted in interviews that young children became unwell first, followed by parents becoming unwell
  - Median age of symptomatic attendees: 7 years (range 1 to 64 years)
  - Epicurve by household suggests secondary household transmission occurring

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Birthday party was held in Perch function room
  - Food mostly catered by Boathouse, additional items brought in by attendees
  - [No clear food source identified from interviews or survey responses]{.red-text}
  - Children consumed a minimal amount of food due to their young age
  - Playpen was used by multiple young children at the party

### Epicurves

**Figure 1** shows an epicurve by symptom onset date.

**Figure 2** shows an epicurve by symptom onset date and time.

**Figure 3** shows an epicurve by symptom onset date and time, and household.

**Figure 4** shows an epicurve by symptom onset date and time, and age group.

:::: {.panel-tabset}

#### Figure 1

**Number of people reporting symptoms, interviewed attendees and survey respondents, by symptom onset date, [`r datetime_update`]{.red-text}**

```{r}
#| fig-align: center
#| out-width: "100%"
#
data_histogram <- linelist_survey %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(symptom_onset_date)) %>% 
  #
  count(symptom_onset_date) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(hover_text = paste0("Symptom onset: ", format(symptom_onset_date, "%d/%m/%Y"), "<br>",
                                    "Cases: ", n))

figure <- data_histogram %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = symptom_onset_date, y = n, text = hover_text)) +
  #
  geom_col(color = "black",
           fill  = "turquoise3",
           width = 1,
           alpha = 1) +
  #
  geom_segment(aes(x = date_event, xend = date_event, y = 0, yend = max(n, na.rm = TRUE)),
               color       = "purple",
               linetype    = "dashed",
               linewidth   = 0.25,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  #
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 day",
               date_labels = "%d-%b") +
  #
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(date_epicurve_start, date_epicurve_end)) +
  #
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA),
                     breaks = scales::breaks_width(1),
                     expand = expansion(add = c(0, 0.1))) +
  #
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = NULL) +
  #
  theme_classic() +
  #
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                   vjust = 0.5,
                                   hjust = 1))

figure %>%
  plotly::ggplotly(tooltip = "text") %>%
  #
  plotly::layout(hovermode = "x",
                 #
                 modebar = list(orientation = 'v'),
                 #
                 xaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Symptom onset date",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 15)),
                 #
                 yaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Number of cases",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 10)))
```

::: {.footnote}
Note:<br>
- The event date is marked with a dashed purple line.
:::

#### Figure 2

**Number of people reporting symptoms, interviewed attendees, by symptom onset date and time, [`r datetime_update`]{.red-text}**

```{r}
#| fig-align: center
#| out-width: "100%"
#
data_histogram <- linelist_clean %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(symptom_onset_datetime)) %>%
  #
  dplyr::group_by(hours_group = lubridate::floor_date(symptom_onset_datetime, "3 hours")) %>% 
  dplyr::count() %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(hover_text = paste0("Symptom onset: ", format(hours_group, "%d/%m/%Y %H:%M"), "<br>",
                                    "Cases: ", n))
 
figure <- data_histogram %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = hours_group, y = n, text = hover_text)) +
  #
  geom_col(color = "black",
           fill  = "turquoise3",
           width = 10800,
           alpha = 1) +
  #
  geom_segment(aes(x = datetime_event, xend = datetime_event, y = 0, yend = 5),
               color       = "purple",
               linetype    = "dashed",
               linewidth   = 0.25,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  #
  scale_x_datetime(breaks = seq(lubridate::floor_date(datetime_epicurve_start, "3 hours"), 
                                datetime_epicurve_end,
                                by = "6 hours"),
                   #
                   date_labels = "%d-%b %H:%M",
                   #
                   expand = c(0, 0)) +
  #
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(datetime_epicurve_start, datetime_epicurve_end)) +
  #
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5),
                     breaks = scales::breaks_width(1),
                     expand = expansion(add = c(0, 0.125))) +
  #
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = NULL) +
  #
  theme_classic() +
  #
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                   vjust = 0.5,
                                   hjust = 1))

figure %>% 
  plotly::ggplotly(tooltip = "text") %>%
  #
  plotly::layout(hovermode = "x",
                 #
                 modebar = list(orientation = 'v'),
                 #
                 xaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Symptom onset date and time",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 20)),
                 #
                 yaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Number of cases",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 10)),
                 #
                 legend = list(x = 0.5,
                               y = -0.25,
                               #
                               orientation = "h",
                               xanchor     = "center"))
```

::: {.footnote}
Note:<br>
- The event start date and time is marked with a dashed purple line.<br>
- 4x cases with unknown symptom onset times have been excluded.
:::

#### Figure 3

**Number of people reporting symptoms, interviewed attendees, by symptom onset date and time and household, [`r datetime_update`]{.red-text}**

```{r}
#| fig-align: center
#| out-width: "100%"
#
data_histogram <- linelist_clean %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(symptom_onset_datetime)) %>%
  #
  dplyr::group_by(hours_group = lubridate::floor_date(symptom_onset_datetime, "3 hours"), household) %>% 
  dplyr::count() %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(hover_text = paste0("Symptom onset: ", format(hours_group, "%d/%m/%Y %H:%M"), "<br>",
                                    "Household: ", household, "<br>",
                                    "Cases: ", n))

figure <- data_histogram %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = hours_group, y = n, fill = household, text = hover_text)) +
  #
  geom_col(color = "black",
           alpha = 1) +
  #
  geom_segment(aes(x = datetime_event, xend = datetime_event, y = 0, yend = 5),
               color       = "purple",
               linetype    = "dashed",
               linewidth   = 0.25,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  #
  scale_x_datetime(breaks = seq(lubridate::floor_date(datetime_epicurve_start, "6 hours"), 
                                datetime_epicurve_end,
                                by = "6 hours"),
                   #
                   date_labels = "%d-%b %H:%M",
                   #
                   expand = c(0, 0)) +
  #
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(datetime_epicurve_start, datetime_epicurve_end)) +
  #
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5),
                     breaks = scales::breaks_width(1),
                     expand = expansion(add = c(0, 0.125))) +
  #
  labs(x    = NULL,
       y    = NULL,
       fill = NULL) +
  #
  theme_classic() +
  #
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                   vjust = 0.5,
                                   hjust = 1))

figure %>% 
  plotly::ggplotly(tooltip = "text") %>%
  #
  plotly::layout(hovermode = "closest",
                 #
                 modebar = list(orientation = 'v'),
                 #
                 xaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Symptom onset date and time",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 20)),
                 #
                 yaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Number of cases",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 10)),
                 #
                 legend = list(x = 0.5,
                               y = -0.55,
                               #
                               orientation = "h",
                               xanchor     = "center"))
```

::: {.footnote}
Note:<br>
- The event start date and time is marked with a dashed purple line.<br>
- 4x cases with unknown symptom onset times have been excluded.
:::

#### Figure 4

**Number of people reporting symptoms, interviewed attendees, by symptom onset date and time and age, [`r datetime_update`]{.red-text}**

```{r}
#| fig-align: center
#| out-width: "100%"
#
data_histogram <- linelist_clean %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(symptom_onset_datetime)) %>%
  #
  dplyr::group_by(hours_group = lubridate::floor_date(symptom_onset_datetime, "3 hours"), age_group) %>% 
  dplyr::count() %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(hover_text = paste0("Symptom onset: ", format(hours_group, "%d/%m/%Y %H:%M"), "<br>",
                                    "Age: ", age_group, "<br>",
                                    "Cases: ", n))

figure <- data_histogram %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = hours_group, y = n, fill = age_group, text = hover_text)) +
  #
  geom_col(color = "black",
           alpha = 1) +
  #
  geom_segment(aes(x = datetime_event, xend = datetime_event, y = 0, yend = 5),
               color       = "purple",
               linetype    = "dashed",
               linewidth   = 0.25,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  #
  scale_x_datetime(breaks = seq(lubridate::floor_date(datetime_epicurve_start, "6 hours"), 
                                datetime_epicurve_end,
                                by = "6 hours"),
                   #
                   date_labels = "%d-%b %H:%M",
                   #
                   expand = c(0, 0)) +
  #
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(datetime_epicurve_start, datetime_epicurve_end)) +
  #
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5),
                     breaks = scales::breaks_width(1),
                     expand = expansion(add = c(0, 0.125))) +
  #
  labs(x    = NULL,
       y    = NULL,
       fill = NULL) +
  #
  theme_classic() +
  #
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
                                   vjust = 0.5,
                                   hjust = 1))

figure %>% 
  plotly::ggplotly(tooltip = "text") %>%
  #
  plotly::layout(hovermode = "closest",
                 #
                 modebar = list(orientation = 'v'),
                 #
                 xaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Symptom onset date and time",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 20)),
                 #
                 yaxis = list(title = list(text     = "Number of cases",
                                           font     = list(size = 13, family = "Arial"),
                                           standoff = 10)),
                 #
                 legend = list(x = 0.5,
                               y = -0.45,
                               #
                               orientation = "h",
                               xanchor     = "center"))
```

::: {.footnote}
Note:<br>
- The event start date and time is marked with a dashed purple line.<br>
- 4x cases with unknown symptom onset times have been excluded.
:::

::::

***

## Environmental Investigations

- Boroondara Council conducted site visit 19/02/2026
  - No ill food handlers or staff reported
  - No food safety or hygiene issues identified

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Deep clean carried out by external cleaning company evening of 18/02/2026
  - Included deep clean of children’s play area and toilets in the Perch function room

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Environmental swabbing conducted 20/02/2026
  - Included benches, chopping boards, knives, meat slicer used for platters
  - [As norovirus has been identified as the causative agent and no bacterial pathogens were isolated from cases, further testing of the environmental swabs will not be required]{.red-text}

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Menu for food served at birthday party and standard menu for Boathouse provided to Council
  - No leftover food available for sampling

<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

- Booking list for day of birthday party and surrounding days provided to Council
  - Further follow-up of booking list not planned at this stage as likely viral gastroenteritis (refer to Risk Assessment section)
  - In addition, booking list consists of >150 individual people who attended on 15/02/2026 so would not be feasible to contact all individuals at this stage
  - Venue does not report illness being reported by other customers

***

## Risk Assessment

- Likely person-person spread of [norovirus]{.red-text}
  - Minimal food consumption by children at the party
  - Children became unwell before parents
  - Likely secondary spread within households (e.g. Household 04)
  - No common food identified by interviewed cases
  - No food safety or hygiene issues identified by Council
  - Six faecal samples collected from [across]{.red-text} three households are [all]{.red-text} negative for bacterial pathogens [and positive for norovirus]{.red-text}

***

## Control Measures

- Refer to Environmental Investigations and Risk Assessment sections

***

## Communications

- Menu-based questionnaire and advice about gastroenteritis disseminated to birthday party attendees via host on 20/02/2026

- Council and DH to be informed via usual processes

- No other broad communications planned at this stage

***

## Further Actions Planned

- [No further actions required]{.red-text}

```{r}
#
knitr::knit_exit()
```

## Additional Analyses

### Demographics

```{r}
# Age distribution, interviewed attendees and survey respondents
linelist_survey %>%
  dplyr::group_by(symptomatic) %>% 
  dplyr::summarise(n      = as.double(n()),
                   min    = round(min(age_years, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 1),
                   max    = round(max(age_years, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 1),
                   mean   = round(mean(age_years, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 1),
                   median = round(median(age_years, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 1)) %>%
  #
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate_if(is.numeric, list(~na_if(., Inf))) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate_if(is.numeric, list(~na_if(., -Inf))) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate_if(is.numeric, list(~na_if(., NaN))) %>% 
  #
  knitr::kable(table.attr = "style = \"color: black;\"",
               align      = "lrrrrr",
               col.names  = c(" ", "n", "Min", "Max", "Mean", "Median")) %>%
  #
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE,
                            html_font  = "Arial",
                            font_size  = 14) %>%
  #
  kableExtra::column_spec(2:5,
                          width = "1.0in") %>% 
  #
  kableExtra::row_spec(row       = 0,
                       bold      = TRUE,
                       extra_css = "border-top: 2px solid lightgray; border-bottom: 2px solid lightgray;") %>%
  #
  kableExtra::row_spec(row       = 2,
                       extra_css = "border-bottom: 2px solid lightgray;") %>% 
  #
  kableExtra::add_header_above(c(" " = 2, "Age (years)" = 4))
```

### Symptoms

```{r}
#
linelist_survey %>% 
  dplyr::summarise(vomiting  = sum(stringr::str_detect(vomiting, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   diarrhoea = sum(stringr::str_detect(diarrhoea, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   fever     = sum(stringr::str_detect(fever, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   nausea    = sum(stringr::str_detect(nausea, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   abdo_pain = sum(stringr::str_detect(abdo_pain, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   headache  = sum(stringr::str_detect(headache, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE),
                   lethargy  = sum(stringr::str_detect(lethargy, "Y"), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  #
  tidyr::pivot_longer(everything(),
                      names_to = "symptom") %>% 
  #
  dplyr::rename(n = value) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(symptom = stringr::str_to_sentence(symptom),
                symptom = stringr::str_replace(symptom, "_", " ")) %>%
  #
  dplyr::mutate(percent = round(n / (nrow(linelist_survey %>% dplyr::filter(symptomatic == "Yes"))) * 100, digits = 0)) %>% 
  #
  dplyr::arrange(desc(percent)) %>% 
  #
  knitr::kable(table.attr = "style = \"color: black;\"",
               align      = "lrr",
               col.names  = c("Symptom", "n", "%")) %>%
  #
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE,
                            html_font  = "Arial",
                            font_size  = 14) %>%
  #
  kableExtra::column_spec(2:3,
                          width = "1.0in") %>%
  #
  kableExtra::row_spec(row       = 0,
                       bold      = TRUE,
                       extra_css = "border-top: 2px solid lightgray; border-bottom: 2px solid lightgray;") %>%
  #
  kableExtra::row_spec(row       = 7,
                       extra_css = "border-bottom: 2px solid lightgray;")
```

### Food History: Cheese Platter

```{r, warning = FALSE}
# Create crosstab
crosstab_platter <- survey_food %>% 
  f_create_crosstab(menu_items = menu_platter)

# Calculate attack rates, relative risks, and odds ratios
crosstab_platter <- crosstab_platter %>% 
  f_calculate_attack()

# Create univariate logistic regression models
models_platter <- survey_food %>% 
  f_create_univariate_model(menu_items = menu_platter)
```


```{r}
# Join crosstab and univariate results into a single, nicely formatted table
crosstab_platter %>% 
  dplyr::left_join(models_platter, by = "menu_item") %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(
    menu_item = dplyr::case_when(
      menu_item == "platter_prosciutto" ~ "Prosciutto",
      menu_item == "platter_bresaola"   ~ "Bresaola",
      menu_item == "platter_cheddar"    ~ "Cheddar cheese",
      menu_item == "platter_blue"       ~ "Blue cheese",
      menu_item == "platter_cornichons" ~ "Cornichons",
      menu_item == "platter_quince"     ~ "Quince paste",
      menu_item == "platter_lavosh"     ~ "Lavosh crackers",
      menu_item == "platter_bark"       ~ "Bark crackers")) %>%
  #
  dplyr::arrange(menu_item, consumed) %>% 
  #
  f_format_table()
```

### Food History: Pizza

```{r, warning = FALSE}
# Create crosstab
crosstab_pizza <- survey_food %>% 
  f_create_crosstab(menu_items = menu_pizza)

# Calculate attack rates, relative risks, and odds ratios
crosstab_pizza <- crosstab_pizza %>% 
  f_calculate_attack()

# Create univariate logistic regression models
models_pizza <- survey_food %>% 
  f_create_univariate_model(menu_items = menu_pizza)
```


```{r}
# Join crosstab and univariate results into a single, nicely formatted table
crosstab_pizza %>% 
  dplyr::left_join(models_pizza, by = "menu_item") %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(
    menu_item = dplyr::case_when(
      menu_item == "pizza_margherita" ~ "Margherita pizza",
      menu_item == "pizza_pumpkin"    ~ "Pumpkin pizza",
      menu_item == "pizza_ham"        ~ "Ham and pineapple pizza",
      menu_item == "pizza_chicken"    ~ "Chicken pizza",
      menu_item == "pizza_prosciutto" ~ "Prosciutto pizza")) %>%
  #
  dplyr::arrange(menu_item, consumed) %>% 
  #
  f_format_table()
```

### Food History: Fruit Platter

```{r, warning = FALSE}
# Create crosstab
crosstab_fruit <- survey_food %>% 
  f_create_crosstab(menu_items = menu_fruit)

# Calculate attack rates, relative risks, and odds ratios
crosstab_fruit <- crosstab_fruit %>% 
  f_calculate_attack()

# Create univariate logistic regression models
models_fruit <- survey_food %>% 
  f_create_univariate_model(menu_items = menu_fruit)
```


```{r}
# Join crosstab and univariate results into a single, nicely formatted table
crosstab_fruit %>% 
  dplyr::left_join(models_fruit, by = "menu_item") %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(
    menu_item = dplyr::case_when(
      menu_item == "fruit_grapes"       ~ "Grapes",
      menu_item == "fruit_strawberries" ~ "Strawberries",
      menu_item == "fruit_watermelon"   ~ "Watermelon",
      menu_item == "fruit_pineapple"    ~ "Pineapple",
      menu_item == "fruit_blueberries"  ~ "Blueberries",
      menu_item == "fruit_raspberries"  ~ "Raspberries")) %>%
  #
  dplyr::arrange(menu_item, consumed) %>% 
  #
  f_format_table()
```

### Food History: Dessert

```{r, warning = FALSE}
# Create crosstab
crosstab_dessert <- survey_food %>% 
  f_create_crosstab(menu_items = menu_dessert)

# Calculate attack rates, relative risks, and odds ratios
crosstab_dessert <- crosstab_dessert %>% 
  f_calculate_attack()

# Create univariate logistic regression models
models_dessert <- survey_food %>% 
  f_create_univariate_model(menu_items = menu_dessert)
```


```{r}
# Join crosstab and univariate results into a single, nicely formatted table
crosstab_dessert %>% 
  dplyr::left_join(models_dessert, by = "menu_item") %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(
    menu_item = dplyr::case_when(
      menu_item == "dessert_tart"        ~ "Blueberry and custard tarts",
      menu_item == "dessert_cake"        ~ "Chocolate cake",
      menu_item == "dessert_crackle"     ~ "Chocolate crackles",
      menu_item == "dessert_donut"       ~ "Jam donuts",
      menu_item == "sorbet_orange"       ~ "Sorbet (blood orange)",
      menu_item == "sorbet_passionfruit" ~ "Sorbet (passionfruit)",
      menu_item == "sorbet_raspberry"    ~ "Sorbet (raspberry)",
      menu_item == "gelato_honey"        ~ "Gelato (honey)",
      menu_item == "gelato_chocolate"    ~ "Gelato (chocolate)",
      menu_item == "gelato_vanilla"      ~ "Gelato (vanilla)")) %>%
  #
  dplyr::arrange(menu_item, consumed) %>% 
  #
  f_format_table()
```

### Food History: Other Foods

```{r, warning = FALSE}
# Create crosstab
crosstab_other <- survey_food %>% 
  f_create_crosstab(menu_items = menu_other)

# Calculate attack rates, relative risks, and odds ratios
crosstab_other <- crosstab_other %>% 
  f_calculate_attack()

# Create univariate logistic regression models
models_other <- survey_food %>% 
  f_create_univariate_model(menu_items = menu_other)
```


```{r}
# Join crosstab and univariate results into a single, nicely formatted table
crosstab_other %>% 
  dplyr::left_join(models_other, by = "menu_item") %>% 
  #
  dplyr::mutate(
    menu_item = dplyr::case_when(
      menu_item == "kids_chicken" ~ "Kids meal (chicken and chips)",
      menu_item == "kids_fish"    ~ "Kids meal (fish and chips)",
      menu_item == "chips"        ~ "Chips",
      menu_item == "drink_juice"  ~ "Drink (juice)",
      menu_item == "drink_other"  ~ "Drink (other)")) %>%
  #
  dplyr::arrange(menu_item, consumed) %>% 
  #
  f_format_table()
```
